repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue duplicate: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference concerns rare factual-event PN/PS ratios, while stat_dose_response_minimax concerns static-dose lower bounds, neither a CAR illness-death shrinking-window EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased gives fixed-window illness-death EIF inference; SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous gives static continuous-exposure effective-sample-size results; neither supplies a CAR multistate bounded-count marked-compound-Poisson experiment.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue duplicate: the closest honest-diameter work is stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference, whose rare PN/PS shell is distinct from Hölder regularization of exact initiation-time survival risk.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI and AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment provide local honest-inference tools, but neither treats learned CAR illness-death EIFs or a bounded-effective-count replacement experiment.

novelty_flags.label: def:eif and prop:intervention-eif-bridge
novelty_flags.one_line: The core attributes its X-dependent displayed EIF to Martinussen--Knudsen--Rytgaard Theorem 3.1, but that theorem is the Section 3 no-baseline-covariate result; the covariate-conditioned EIF is Theorem 4.1.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-causal-validity and both open-ended questions
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Correspondence between the observed-filtration hazard intervention and the full-filtration causal intervention is assumed rather than identified from observed trajectories.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All causal-target and inference claims are conditional on existence of a compatible full-data extension satisfying the stated observed-hazard causal-validity restriction.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Theorem 3.1 gives the observed-data EIF in the no-baseline-covariate Section 3 setting. Section 4 then conditions the transition hazards on X, and Theorem 4.1 supplies the corresponding covariate EIF by replacing the Theorem 3.1 quantities with their X-conditional versions.
The paper studies exponentially tilted static continuous-exposure interventions, derives an EIF and efficiency bound, and gives effective-sample-size n/delta minimax and estimator-rate results.

Theorem 2.1 gives Gaussian approximation for suprema of empirical processes under stated measurability, moment, entropy, and variance conditions; it does not derive a rare-event complement experiment.
The paper proposes weighted local-linear estimation of a general continuous-treatment response, a uniform Bahadur representation, bootstrap bands, undersmoothing selection, and bias-control bands.
The paper develops kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment estimation, asymptotic theory, and data-driven bandwidth selection.
The paper gives kernel-localized cross-fitted doubly robust continuous-treatment inference under high-level nuisance conditions, rather than multistate CAR survival inference.
The paper studies global and local dose-response estimators, including a higher-order influence-function estimator and mean-square-error upper bounds.
The article derives minimax rates and a local-polynomial estimator for Hölder-smooth CATE estimation, including a localized lower-bound construction.

The paper gives cross-fitted doubly robust treatment-specific survival-curve inference with pointwise and uniform asymptotic linearity, for baseline treatment rather than a shrinking initiation hazard.

source_verification_receipts.locator: Publisher version inaccessible to this review connector; source verification required before relying on any theorem- or formula-level comparison.
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: No formula-level mismatch is asserted from this inaccessible primary source; the proposal's broad local-regression comparison is not used to support a flag.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the Theorem 3.1-only EIF anchor with an explicit Section 4/Theorem 4.1 covariate anchor, and state that Theorem 3.1 is only the no-covariate building block.

Make the Gaussian branch a first-result target by naming primitive localized nuisance-product and inverse-information conditions, an admissible bandwidth range, and the resulting pointwise or uniform limit law.
Specify one triangular bounded-hazard submodel for the critical branch, including its initiation intensity, mark law, compensator, and the Laplace-functional convergence it must establish.

recommended_next_step: Correct the covariate-EIF comparator anchor, retain the accepted causal-validity scope condition, and then advance the two field-tier open kernels to Stage 0.
